Seal status

ABSTAIN_INSUFFICIENT_SCOPE · Chain verified (not externally authenticated)
Assembly attestation: EPHEMERAL_UNVERIFIABLE
The run found evidence, but one or more source boundaries were not verified.
Source coverage: 167 analyzed / 167 eligible source; discovery accounting: 343 discovered

Detector scope

Detector scope: FORGE modeled only these families: atomic-state-mutation, command-injection, decision-adjacent-float, deterministic-core, dynamic-evaluation, hardcoded-credential, honest-degradation, money-as-float, parser-boundary, path-traversal, sql-aggregation-not-materialization, sql-injection, subprocess, tamper-evident-audit-chain, unsafe-deserialization, unverified-webhook, unversioned-serialization, validate-at-the-boundary. It did not analyze defect classes outside that list, including general business logic, business authorization, concurrency and race conditions, general type errors, resource lifetime and leak analysis, cross-message temporal and stateful behavioral sequences.

A clean disposition means no surviving finding within both the declared source scope and detector scope. It is not a repository-wide correctness or safety certification.

Findings

vigia/core/entanglement.py

MEDIUM

Agent: bug_investigator · Category: INFERRED · Outcome: OBSERVED

The decision comparison `return score >= 2.0, max(1, len(strong)), min(1.0, score / 5.0)` at vigia/core/entanglement.py:505 uses a binary float threshold, so rounding at the boundary may flip the result.

vigia/core/entanglement.py:505: return score >= 2.0, max(1, len(strong)), min(1.0, score / 5.0)
Review actions

Source location: vigia/core/entanglement.py:505

Reproduce this audit:

forge audit /tmp/forge-vigia-production-view --output forge-run
Reasoning chain
Observed construct matches; induction was undetermined: No supported float(x) comparison shape was found for differential induction.

💬 Narrated summary (not verified)

Reader-oriented summary

An internally consistent hash-chain finding set records 212 surviving finding(s): 125 medium, 87 low. The highest recorded severity is medium. The chain is not externally authenticated. The findings were emitted by bug_investigator, deterministic-core, honest-degradation, and 5 more. Affected module(s): vigia/core/atomic_io.py, vigia/core/audit_action.py, vigia/core/bundle_builder.py, and 37 more. Reported observations include: The decision comparison `return score >= 2.0, max(1, len(strong)), min(1.0, score / 5.0)` at vigia/core/entanglement.py:505 uses a binary float threshold, so rounding at the boundary may flip the result., The parser call `data = json.load(f)` at vigia/core/lr_calibration.py:490 has no nearby exception handling, so malformed input may escape as an opaque failure., The dynamic command invocation `proc = subprocess.run(` at vigia/pipeline/pipeline.py:1165 may pass attacker-controlled arguments without an enclosing failure boundary., and 209 more.

This deterministic prose is derived after verification from the sealed finding set only. It is not evidence, is not sealed, and cannot change a finding, severity, disposition, or audit decision.

Limitations

  • The seal proves findings were not altered after sealing.
  • The seal does not prove findings are correct.
  • A full-access attacker can forge a consistent replacement chain from scratch.
